title: blechnum australe var. triloba
intro: Blechnum australe var. triloba, also known as the Three-lobed Chain Fern or Austral Chain Fern, is a variety of the Blechnum australe species, a type of fern native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Blechnum australe var. triloba include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea or tincture, and its leaves can also be infused in hot water to make a medicinal drink.
